meaning: to be angry at someone; to yell at someone out of anger

expressions.english: something to be upset about
Don't get angry.; Don't be upset.
I wasn't angry.
expressions.notes: -(으)ㄴ 적 없다 = to have never + p.p. (in this context, -(으)ㄴ 적 없다 means "No, I didn't.")

expressions.english: I don't want to get angry.
expressions.notes: -고 싶지 않다 = to not want to + V

expressions.english: I think I would be angry, too.
That's nothing to get upset about.
expressions.notes:
그건 = that (subject)
그건 is the contracted form of 그것은.
화날 일 = something to be upset about

expressions.english:
He gets angry easily.
He loses his temper easily.
expressions.notes:
쉽다 = to be easy
쉽게 is an adverb form of 쉽다.

expressions.english: If you keep acting like that, I will get mad.
expressions.notes:
계속 = repeatedly, again and again, continuously
그러다 = to act like that, to behave like that

expressions.english: Stop being upset (about it) and lighten up a bit.
expressions.notes:
그만 -고 = stop + V-ing and
이제 = now
기분 풀다 = to relieve anger in a mental (not physical) way; to calm down; to lighten up

expressions.korean: 부탁 안 들어 주면 화날 거예요?
expressions.english: If I refuse your request, will you get angry (at me)?
expressions.notes:
부탁 = favor
부탁을 들어주다 = to grant one's favor

expressions.english:
Getting mad about it won't help solve the problem (for you).
Nothing changes, even if you get mad (at me).
expressions.notes:
-다고 = just because; saying that S + V
해결되다 = to be solved

expressions.korean: 그 사람이 화내는 걸 처음 봤어요.
expressions.english: I've never seen him get angry before.
expressions.notes: 처음 = first; for the first time

expressions.english: Is that something that you need to get so upset about?
expressions.notes:
그게 = that, that thing (subject)
그게 is the contracted form of 그것은.
그렇게 = like that; such + adjective/adverb
싶하게 is an adverb form of 싶하다.
싶하다 = to be excessive, too much; to be harsh

expressions.korean: 집에 늦게 가면 엄마가 화날 거예요.
expressions.english: If I go home late, my mom will get mad.
expressions.notes:
늦다 = to be late
늦게 is the adverb form of 늦다.
엄마 = mom

expressions.korean: 제가 뭘 잘못 했길래 그렇게 화내요?
expressions.english: What have I done (to you) to make you get angry at me?
expressions.notes:
뭘 = what (object)
뭘 is the contracted form of 무엇을.
잘못하다 = to do something wrong; to do something bad to someone
-길래 = for what reason, resulting from which situation

expressions.korean: 그 사람은 사소한 일에도 맨날 화내요.
expressions.english: He always gets mad, even over small things.
expressions.notes:
사소하다 = to be trivial, minor, petty
사소한 is the adjective form of 사소하다.
맨날 = everyday; all the time

expressions.korean: 화내지 말고 제 이야기를 들어 보세요.
expressions.english: Don't get angry, but try listening to me.
I was late, so my friend got mad at me.

expressions.korean: 저희 아버지는 화내실 때 정말 무서워요.
expressions.english: When my father gets angry, he is really scary.
expressions.notes:
저희 = (polite) we; us; our
아버지 = father
무섭다 = to be scary; to be scared

expressions.korean: 무조건 화내지 말고, 어떻게 할지 생각해 봐요.
expressions.english: Don't get mad without thinking, let's just think about how we can figure this out.
expressions.notes:
무조건 = no matter what, unconditionally, without considering other factors
-이/이/여요 = imperative; Let's + V
